task_completion.description: Verify a new channel named 'ADT_Inbound_Validator' was created.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a channel named 'ADT_Inbound_Validator' in the NextGen Connect (Mirth Connect) Administrator channels list or via an API deployment script.

task_completion.description: Verify the channel source is configured to listen on TCP port 6661.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look at the Source tab or configuration showing the connector type as 'TCP Listener' and the port set to '6661'.

task_completion.description: Verify the source data type is HL7 v2.x with MLLP framing.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for 'HL7 v2.x' selected as the inbound data type and transmission mode/framing set to 'MLLP' in the source properties.

task_completion.description: Verify the channel validates the presence of PID.3 and PID.5.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a Filter, Transformer, or Script (e.g., JavaScript) that explicitly checks for the existence/length of the HL7 fields PID.3 (Patient ID) and PID.5 (Patient Name).

task_completion.description: Verify the channel returns an ACK with MSA-1='AA' for valid messages and MSA-1='AE' for invalid messages.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for response generation logic (in scripts or ACK settings) that conditionally sets the MSA-1 segment to 'AA' when PID.3 and PID.5 are present, and 'AE' when either is missing.

task_completion.description: Verify valid messages are written to a file writer destination at '/home/ga/hl7_outbound/'.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look at the Destinations tab showing a 'File Writer' connector type with the Directory set to '/home/ga/hl7_outbound/'.

task_completion.description: Verify the channel is deployed and active.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look at the NextGen Connect Dashboard showing the 'ADT_Inbound_Validator' channel with a status of 'Deployed' or 'Started'.

integrity.description: Verify the agent used NextGen Connect (via GUI or API) rather than building a standalone mock TCP server.
integrity.visual_evidence: Look for screenshots of the NextGen Connect Administrator application, or terminal windows showing API curl commands/scripts interacting with https://localhost:8443/api.

integrity.description: Verify the agent did not manually create fake HL7 output files to bypass the channel logic.
integrity.visual_evidence: Ensure there are no terminal commands like 'echo ... > /home/ga/hl7_outbound/msg.hl7' used to fake the destination output.

integrity.description: Verify the validation and ACK logic is integrated into the channel configuration.
integrity.visual_evidence: The logic checking PID.3/PID.5 and setting AA/AE should be visible inside the NextGen Connect interface (e.g., Edit Channel -> Scripts/Filters) or within the XML/JSON payload sent to the API, not just printed in a text editor.

privileged_info_for_vlm: HL7 v2.x is a standard healthcare messaging protocol. PID.3 (Patient Identifier) and PID.5 (Patient Name) are standard fields in the PID segment. In acknowledgement messages, the MSA segment contains the status: MSA-1='AA' indicates Application Accept (success), and MSA-1='AE' indicates Application Error. MLLP (Minimal Lower Layer Protocol) is the standard transport framing. NextGen Connect (formerly Mirth Connect) is the integration platform used for this task.
